channel.title: Brazil Elections 2026
channel.description: Real-time monitoring of the 2026 Brazilian presidential elections.

item.title: Entenda a broncopneumonia, doença que levou Bolsonaro à UTI
item.description: Former President Jair Bolsonaro was admitted to the ICU with bacterial bronchopneumonia, exhibiting high fever, dropping oxygen saturation, and respiratory distress. His son, Senator Flávio Bolsonaro, attributed the hospitalization to aspiration of gastric fluids during hiccups, linking the severity to prison conditions and requesting house arrest, though medical experts stress that such a causal link cannot be confirmed with certainty.

item.title: Quem é Darren Beattie, assessor de Trump que teve visita a Bolsonaro negada
item.description: Darren Beattie, a Donald Trump advisor appointed to oversee Brazil-related affairs, had his U.S. visa revoked and was denied entry to visit former Brazilian President Jair Bolsonaro. Beattie is a known critic of the Lula government and Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes, and has faced accusations of racism and sexism over social media posts. Lula stated the ban was a retaliatory measure for the U.S. denying visas to Brazilian Health Minister Alexandre Padilha amid Trump-era sanctions against Brazil.

item.title: A ressalva de Fux no julgamento que confirmou Moro como réu
item.description: Supreme Court Justice Luiz Fux voted to reject Sergio Moro's motion for clarification, upholding Moro's status as a defendant in a slander case against Justice Gilmar Mendes. While Fux agreed with the defense that the source of the leaked video and Moro's knowledge of it should be investigated, he maintained his vote to deny the appeal. All justices have now voted in the virtual session, which resumed after Fux previously requested a review when the vote was 4-0 against Moro.

item.title: “Não tenho dúvida da lealdade de Eduardo Paes”, diz presidente do PT
item.description: The national president of the PT, Edinho Silva, stated that Rio de Janeiro’s mayor, Eduardo Paes (PSD), will remain loyal to President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva (PL) in the 2026 electoral campaign, despite Paes being a pre-candidate for state governor. Edinho also expressed confidence in Lula’s strong performance in São Paulo and reaffirmed that the Northeast remains the president’s main electoral base due to the social transformations of his administration. The remarks were made in a CNN Brasil interview, following Lula’s visit to the Federal Hospital of Andaraí in Rio, attended by Paes.

item.title: Genial/Quaest: Apoio de Trump a Flávio Bolsonaro beneficiaria mais Lula do que o próprio senador
item.description: The Genial/Quaest poll finds that Donald Trump’s endorsement of Flávio Bolsonaro would more likely boost votes for Lula (32%) than for Bolsonaro himself (28%). Among Lula supporters, 79% believe the endorsement would help Lula, while 80% of Bolsonaro supporters think it would aid Flávio. The effect is fragmented among independents and varies by religion, with Catholics favoring Lula and evangelicals leaning toward Bolsonaro.

item.title: O silêncio dos EUA sobre Paulo Figueiredo, denunciado pela trama golpista
item.description: Brazilian authorities have failed to notify bolsonarist blogger Paulo Figueiredo, indicted by the PGR for involvement in the 2022 coup plot, as U.S. authorities have not responded to multiple judicial requests and letters rogatory. The lack of U.S. cooperation hinders the legal process against Figueiredo, grandson of former dictator João Figueiredo, who resides in the U.S. and is accused of spreading disinformation to keep Bolsonaro in power after his 2022 election loss.

item.title: Missão Apoio
item.description: The Rede party decided to maintain its federation with PSOL after rejecting a potential union with the PT, defying internal pressure led by figures like Guilherme Boulos to move closer to the Workers' Party. The decision reinforces a strategic alliance with PSOL, distancing itself from the PT. This reflects ideological tensions and strategic positioning within the Brazilian left ahead of the 2026 elections.

item.title: É um erro oposição se alinhar a Trump em detrimento do Brasil, diz PT
item.description: PT national president Edinho Silva criticized the opposition for aligning with the U.S. government under Donald Trump, claiming it harms Brazilian interests, particularly due to U.S. tariffs on Brazilian goods. He emphasized that Lula focuses on domestic priorities, including diesel price controls and aid to flood victims in Minas Gerais. Silva argued that Trump’s aggressiveness undermines the UN and has made national sovereignty a global debate.

item.title: Clica e Confirma ressalta lançamento da página das Eleições 2026
item.description: The Clica e Confirma program highlighted the launch of the official 2026 Elections website by the TSE, providing information for voters, parties, and candidates, along with audiovisual materials from Secom. It also covered the first edition of the 'Ecocidadania' project in Tocantins, which offered electoral services to recyclable material collectors, and two speeches by Minister Cármen Lúcia on AI, democracy, and combating gender-based violence at the TCU and UDF.

item.title: Lula em Washington fica sem data e caso Beattie amplia tensão com EUA
item.description: The meeting between President Lula and Donald Trump in Washington remains unscheduled for March, with a possible rescheduling only from April onward due to conflicting agendas and rising bilateral tensions. The delay is influenced by the Iran conflict, the Beattie case, the Section 301 investigation, and U.S. pressure to designate Brazilian criminal factions as terrorists, which Brazil resists. Despite disagreements, both governments express interest in cooperation on police intelligence sharing and combating money laundering via cryptocurrencies.

item.title: Depois do diesel, preço da gasolina também vai subir? Entenda os possíveis impactos econômicos no Brasil da guerra no Irã
item.description: The Iran war risks driving up global oil prices, with Brent exceeding $100 per barrel and WTI above R$ 98, pressuring fuel costs in Brazil. Petrobras has already raised diesel A by R$ 0.38, and gasoline prices are expected to follow, depending on the conflict's duration and potential closure of the Strait of Hormuz. The Brazilian government is assessing scenarios to mitigate impacts on inflation, interest rates, and public finances.

item.title: Mendonça articulou formação rápida de maioria na 2ª Turma para manter prisão de Vorcaro em meio à discussão de delação
item.description: Supreme Court Minister André Mendonça secured a quick majority in the Second Chamber to maintain the preventive detention of banker Daniel Vorcaro, amid pressure for his release in exchange for a plea bargain. The move was driven by fears that Vorcaro could reveal information implicating authorities, particularly from the Centrão, in an election year. Vorcaro’s defense denied any plea bargain initiative, calling the reports 'untrue'.

item.title: Estão brincando com a saúde de Bolsonaro, diz Flávio
item.description: Flávio Bolsonaro criticized authorities for not granting humanitarian house arrest to his father, Jair Bolsonaro, saying they are 'playing with his health'. Jair Bolsonaro was transferred from prison to DF Star hospital in Brasília after showing chills and vomiting, and was admitted to the ICU with bilateral bacterial bronchopneumonia, a condition described as serious by doctors.

item.title: PSD se aproxima de anunciar Ratinho Jr. como candidato; decisão será confirmada no fim do mês
item.description: The PSD is close to announcing Paraná Governor Ratinho Júnior as its presidential candidate for the 2026 elections. The decision is expected to be confirmed by the end of this month, according to party leaders speaking to Estadão.

item.title: O fantasma que assombra o bolso dos brasileiros e o governo Lula em tempos de guerra
item.description: President Lula stated that the government is preparing tax measures and agreements with states to mitigate the impact of the Iran war on fuel prices and prevent domestic inflation, especially in food costs. The strategy includes tax adjustments, financial compensation, and possible ICMS reductions by states to avoid passing on international oil price increases to consumers, with the goal of preventing diesel hikes from pressuring the entire price chain and reaching 'the Brazilian’s bean plate'. In an election year, containing inflation is a priority to protect government popularity among low-income voters.

item.title: Veja números de Endrick no Lyon e marcas quebradas no clube francês
item.description: Endrick, the forward loaned by Real Madrid to Lyon in 2026, made an immediate impact in French football: in 11 appearances, he scored six goals and provided four assists, leading the club in direct goal contributions since his debut. He broke personal records, won Ligue 1 Player of the Month, and became the first player to score in all his competition debuts for Lyon (Ligue 1, Coupe de France, and Europa League).

item.title: Bolsonaro deve ficar ao menos 7 dias no hospital, diz médico
item.description: Former President Jair Bolsonaro was admitted to the ICU at DF Star Hospital after experiencing discomfort at the Papudinha detention facility in Brasília. Cardiologist Brasil Caiado stated he will likely remain hospitalized for at least seven days due to severe bilateral bronchopneumonia requiring intravenous antibiotics. Senator Flávio Bolsonaro confirmed that Bolsonaro woke up with chills and vomiting prior to admission.

item.title: Guerra com Irã: Pesquisa mostra qual a visão dos brasileiros sobre as ações dos EUA
item.description: The Genial/Quaest poll shows 48% of Brazilians hold an unfavorable view of the U.S., down from 56% in October 2023, due to U.S. bombings in Iran alongside Israel. Disapproval is highest among non-Lulist leftists (77%) and Lula supporters (63%), while 68% of Bolsonarists view the U.S. favorably. Explicit Trump support for Flávio Bolsonaro would increase Lula’s vote chance for 32%, versus 28% for Bolsonaro.

item.title: Imprensa internacional repercute cancelamento de visto de assessor de Trump por Lula
item.description: The Lula government revoked the visa of U.S. State Department advisor Darren Beattie after Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes blocked his visit to jailed ex-president Jair Bolsonaro. International outlets framed the move as a new diplomatic rift between Brazil and the U.S., occurring amid Brazil’s 2026 presidential race where Lula seeks re-election against Flávio Bolsonaro as his main opponent.

item.title: Milícia, rachadinha e Comando Vermelho: a artilharia pesada de aliados de Lula contra Flávio Bolsonaro
item.description: Lula's government ministers, including Gleisi Hoffmann and Guilherme Boulos, launched heavy attacks against Flávio Bolsonaro, linking him to Rio de Janeiro's militias, salary kickback schemes, and the Comando Vermelho via a 30-second Instagram video after he tied with Lula in polls. The strategy aims to reverse the polling tie without destroying his candidacy, potentially opening the door for Tarcísio de Freitas as a replacement. Flávio and Bolsonarist allies retaliate, accusing the Lula government of corruption and claiming the PT is deploying its 'candidate-grinding machine'.

item.title: Governo diz que respondeu 'na mesma moeda' ao revogar visto e não acredita em desgaste com Trump
item.description: The Brazilian government revoked the visa of Darren Beattie, a Donald Trump adviser, as a proportional response to the U.S. policy that denies visas to individuals who provide false information. Officials from the Lula administration say the move is unlikely to cause major strain in U.S.-Brazil relations, although they acknowledge the meeting between Lula and Trump may not take place. The revocation is seen as the minimum expected reaction given the U.S. stance.

item.title: Policiais rodoviários ameaçam paralisação por fundo contra o crime
item.description: Federal highway police threaten to halt operations if the Lula government does not advance the creation of the National Fund to Combat Organized Crime (Funcoc), which was removed from the Anti-Facction Bill by rapporteur Guilherme Derrite. The PRF federation says it has requested a meeting with Justice Minister Wellington César Lima without response and has declared a 'state of alert,' warning that future measures are not ruled out.

item.title: Crusoé: Apoio de Trump ajudaria ou atrapalharia Flávio?
item.description: A Genial/Quaest poll released on Friday, the 13th, found that 28% of respondents believe Donald Trump’s support would increase Flávio Bolsonaro’s chances of winning the 2026 presidential election, while 32% think such an endorsement would hurt his campaign. The survey also assessed the potential impact of Trump’s backing on President Lula’s (PT) candidacy.

item.title: Ouvidoria em Foco: unidade adota ações voltadas a mulheres quilombolas vítimas de violência política
item.description: In November 2025, the TSE’s Women’s Ombudswoman launched an initiative to support quilombola women victims of political violence, promoting active listening in communities and proposing measures to expand voting access with sensitivity to diversity. Actions include forming a working group to design affirmative policies and forwarding recommendations to Regional Electoral Courts (TREs).

item.title: Sessão presencial do STF vai analisar decisão sobre sigilos de Lulinha
item.description: Supreme Court Minister Gilmar Mendes requested a spotlight on the judgment reviewing Justice Flávio Dino's decision to suspend the breaking of bank secrecy approved by the INSS Parliamentary Inquiry Committee, which affected businessman Fábio Luís Lula da Silva (Lulinha), son of President Lula. The case will be heard in a plenary session. Also highlighted in VEJA's Giro are the court's decision on Daniel Vorcaro's arrest and the hospitalization of former President Jair Bolsonaro.

item.title: Paes anuncia caneta emagrecedora na rede de saúde do Rio e Lula diz que tema é 'delicado'
item.description: Rio de Janeiro Mayor Eduardo Paes announced that starting next week, the municipal health network will distribute weight-loss pens (Ozempic), beginning with the inauguration of the West Zone Super Health Center on Tuesday (17). President Lula, present at the event, called the topic 'delicate,' stressing that doctors must first guide patients toward healthy eating before prescribing weight-loss medication. Paes defended the policy as a fulfillment of his 2024 campaign promise, citing his own 30 kg weight loss using the drug.

item.title: Imprensa internacional repercute cancelamento de visto de assessor de Trump pelo Brasil
item.description: The Lula administration revoked the visa of U.S. State Department advisor Darren Beattie after Supreme Court Minister Alexandre de Moraes blocked his planned visit to jailed former President Jair Bolsonaro at the Papuda penitentiary. The move, reported by Reuters, Washington Post, and Bloomberg, is seen as a new diplomatic spat in a broader pattern of bilateral tensions dating back to August 2025, occurring amid Brazil’s presidential election campaign where Lula seeks re-election against Flávio Bolsonaro as a main rival.

item.title: Paulistas culpam Enel mais que Tarcísio, Lula e Nunes por falta de energia, diz Datafolha
item.description: A Datafolha survey across 71 municipalities in São Paulo state found that 49% of respondents blame the power outages on Enel, significantly more than the state government (16%, Tarcísio), federal government (14%, Lula), or municipal administration (6%, Nunes). Combined, criticism of all three government levels (36%) falls short of the direct blame assigned to the company. Enel claims the result reflects improvement compared to earlier surveys limited to the capital city.

item.title: Guerra no Irã: MP reduz preço do diesel para enfrentar alta do petróleo
item.description: The government issued MP 1.340/2026 to cut diesel prices by R$0.64 per liter through tax cuts and producer/importer subsidies, valid until December 31, 2026. The measure aims to shield consumers from the impact of the Iran war on global oil prices by zeroing PIS/Cofins on diesel and imposing fines of R$50,000 to R$500 million for price abuses. To offset up to R$30 billion in lost revenue, the government will introduce 12% export taxes on oil and 50% on diesel, while strengthening penalties under the National Fuels Supply Law.

item.title: Groenlândia vive crise política com renúncia de ministra em meio a tensão diplomática com EUA
item.description: Greenland's Foreign Minister Vivian Motzfeldt resigned after her party, Siumut, withdrew from the governing coalition led by Prime Minister Jens-Frederik Nielsen. The resignation comes amid heightened diplomatic tensions with the United States, where President Donald Trump has repeatedly expressed interest in acquiring the Arctic territory. Nielsen will temporarily take over her duties while striving to maintain a broad coalition to counter external pressures.

item.title: Apoio de Trump a Flávio beneficiaria mais Lula que senador, aponta Genial/Quaest
item.description: A Genial/Quaest poll finds that a potential endorsement of Flávio Bolsonaro by Donald Trump would benefit Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva more (32%) than the senator himself (28%). Among independent voters, 19% say the backing would increase their likelihood to vote for Lula, compared to 16% for Flávio.

item.title: Em ano eleitoral, Lula revoga visto de assessor dos EUA que queria visitar Bolsonaro
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva (PT) revoked the entry visa of a senior American diplomat who sought to visit former President Jair Bolsonaro (PL) in prison. The move was intended to prevent potential foreign interference during Brazil's 2026 electoral year, according to the Brazilian government's justification.

item.title: Trama golpista: Moraes determina prisão de últimos réus livres do núcleo 3, composto pelos 'kids pretos'
item.description: Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes ordered the arrest of the remaining free members of the 'kids pretos' group, a cell of special forces military personnel who plotted to assassinate Lula, Alckmin, and Moraes in 2022 to keep Bolsonaro in power after his electoral defeat. Sentences range from 1 year and 11 months to 24 years, with most to be served in closed regime. The First Chamber of the STF unanimously upheld the convictions in November 2023.

item.title: É #FAKE que governo Lula quer taxar entregadores de aplicativo em R$ 10 por corrida
item.description: It is false that the Lula government wants to tax app delivery workers R$10 per trip. The proposal under discussion in Congress establishes a minimum wage floor of R$8.50 to R$10 per delivery, to be paid by platforms to workers—not a charge on them. The Presidency’s Secretariat of Communication confirmed there is no intent to create a new tax on this category.

item.title: Governo deve rejeitar proposta de Trump para Brasil receber estrangeiros capturados nos EUA
item.description: The Lula government should reject the U.S. proposal for Brazil to house foreigners captured in the U.S. in its prisons, along with other demands such as sharing biometric data of refugees and presenting a plan to eliminate the PCC and CV. Brazilian diplomats argue the proposal violates the LGPD, lacks legal basis in Brazilian law, and that Brazil already has structured actions and existing cooperation with the U.S. to combat organized crime.

item.title: O melhor para Lula seria Trump retaliar o cancelamento de visto do americano
item.description: President Lula seeks to regain popularity by confronting Donald Trump's measures, including the potential cancellation of visa for Trump advisor Darren Beattie, to reinforce a nationalist sovereignty narrative. The strategy relies on a hostile Trump reaction to frame Bolsonaro allies as subservient to the U.S., though the ongoing Banco Master scandal may diminish its impact. Genial/Quaest research shows rising anti-American sentiment in Brazil, with favorable views of the U.S. falling from 44% to 38%.

item.title: Sem Eduardo e Zambelli, PL monta estratégia para buscar novos puxadores de voto em 2026
item.description: The PL lost two of its main vote-getters for the 2026 election: Eduardo Bolsonaro, residing in the U.S. and facing trial at the STF, and Carla Zambelli, imprisoned in Italy and ineligible for 8 years. The party is now seeking new candidates to maintain its electoral strength, focusing on the Southeast region — particularly São Paulo, Minas Gerais, and Rio de Janeiro — with bets on Nikolas Ferreira and Sóstenes Cavalcante.

item.title: Rio: Tribunal de Justiça concede habeas corpus a vereador Salvino Oliveira
item.description: The Rio de Janeiro Court of Justice granted habeas corpus to councilman Salvino Oliveira (PSD), releasing him after his arrest last Wednesday (11). He was targeted in the Red Legacy operation, accused of negotiating with trafficker Edgar Alves de Andrade, known as 'Doca', a leader of the Red Command, to obtain permission for electoral campaigning in the Gardênia Azul community. Oliveira, 28 and in his first term, claimed he was a victim of 'a fight that is not mine'.

item.title: Crusoé: Relação com Brasil “não vai melhorar até eleições”, diz cônsul israelense
item.description: Israel's consul-general in São Paulo, Rafael Erdreich, stated that diplomatic relations between Brazil and Israel are at their lowest possible level and will not improve until the 2026 Brazilian elections. While acknowledging the historical partnership between the two nations, he criticized the Lula government's stance on the Iran war and the Hamas conflict. The remarks were made in an exclusive interview with journalists and reported by Crusoé.

item.title: O impacto de eventual apoio de Trump a Flávio Bolsonaro, segundo a Quaest
item.description: A Quaest poll suggests that potential endorsement of Flávio Bolsonaro by Donald Trump could harm his presidential bid, with 32% of respondents saying it would increase their likelihood to vote for Lula, compared to 28% who believe it would help Bolsonaro. Additionally, unfavorable views of the U.S. remain high, with 48% of Brazilians holding a negative opinion of the United States, versus 38% favorable.

item.title: O que há num sobrenome?
item.description: The article examines FlÃ¡vio Bolsonaro's candidacy as a tool of right-wing control by Jair Bolsonaro, highlighting debates over whether he represents a 'moderate' or reactionary Bolsonarism. Experts discuss if his candidacy reflects moderation or embodies a reactionary tendency linked to goals such as amnesty for coup plotters, impeachment of STF justices, and alignment with Trump, raising concerns for Brazilian democracy.

item.title: Caso sobre quebra de sigilo de Lulinha será analisado por plenário do STF após pedido de Gilmar
item.description: Minister Gilmar Mendes requested a spotlight on the STF's judgment regarding the suspension of the approvals by the INSS Parliamentary Inquiry Committee (CPI), including the banking and fiscal data breach of Lulinha, son of President Lula. The judgment, previously conducted virtually, will now be resumed in the physical plenary session, with a date to be set by STF President Edson Fachin. So far, only Minister Flávio Dino has voted, upholding his suspension of the data breaches, arguing that the 'bloc' voting of 87 requests fails to meet constitutional and legal requirements.

item.title: Cleitinho lidera disputa ao governo de Minas com até 40% das intenções
item.description: Senator Cleitinho Azevedo leads all vote intention scenarios for the Minas Gerais gubernatorial race in 2026, reaching up to 40% in one simulation according to Real Time Big Data. Senator Rodrigo Pacheco consistently ranks second, while Alexandre Kalil and Mateus Simões show strong support. In scenarios excluding Cleitinho, either Pacheco or Kalil take the lead, suggesting a polarized contest if he does not run.

item.title: “Nossa pressão deu certo”, afirma Zema sobre maioria no STF
item.description: Minas Gerais Governor Romeu Zema (Novo) celebrated the Second Chamber of the STF's decision to maintain the preventive detention of banker Daniel Vorcaro, controller of Banco Master, stating 'our pressure worked'. He attributed the outcome to public mobilization around the case.

item.title: Petrobras aumenta preço do diesel após Lula zerar impostos
item.description: Petrobras announced a R$ 0.38 per liter increase in diesel A prices for distributors, effective Saturday, the 14th. The hike came one day after President Lula zeroed PIS and Cofins taxes on diesel nationwide. The move aims to offset federal revenue loss from the tax exemption.

item.title: Lula revoga visto de diplomata do governo Trump que queria visitar Bolsonaro
item.description: President Lula revoked the visa of Darren Beattie, a senior U.S. State Department official for Brazil, who sought to visit imprisoned ex-president Bolsonaro over coup attempt charges. The STF had previously denied the visit, citing concerns of foreign interference in an election year. The visa, originally granted for a critical minerals forum in São Paulo, was canceled due to omitted information and false statements about the visit's true purpose.

item.title: Quaest: 28% dizem que apoio de Trump a Flávio Bolsonaro aumentam chance de votar no senador; 32% dizem que aumentam chance de votar em Lula
item.description: Quaest poll shows 32% of Brazilians believe Donald Trump's support for Flávio Bolsonaro would increase their likelihood of voting for Lula, compared to 28% who say it would boost support for Bolsonaro. The effect is polarized: Trump's backing strengthens Lula among his base (79%) and Bolsonaro among his (80%), while independents and non-religious voters lean toward third-way candidates.

item.title: A reação nos bastidores do governo Lula ao encontro de emissário de Trump com Bolsonaro
item.description: An emissary linked to Donald Trump attempted to visit jailed former president Jair Bolsonaro, initially approved by Supreme Court Minister Alexandre de Moraes but later blocked due to concerns over foreign interference in Brazil’s political process. The Lula administration viewed the move as a politically charged gesture with potential electoral impact and a dangerous precedent for external influence, sparking diplomatic discomfort with Brazil’s Foreign Ministry. Bolsonaro’s hospitalization for bronchopneumonia halted any prison-based political activity, but Lula used the incident to draw parallels with recent restrictions on Brazilian officials entering the U.S., escalating bilateral tensions.

item.title: Governo avalia que assessor de Trump omitiu real motivo de visita e transformou viagem ao Brasil em ato político
item.description: The Lula government assesses that Donald Trump’s advisor on Brazil affairs, Darren Beattie, concealed the real purpose of his visit to Brazil, turning it into a political act by scheduling undeclared meetings, including a request to visit jailed former president Bolsonaro. The revocation of Beattie’s visa was justified on the principle of reciprocity, countering efforts to forge a bolsonarista-trumpista alliance ahead of the 2026 presidential election.

item.title: Lula diz que proibiu assessor do governo Trump de entrar no Brasil
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva barred Darren Beattie, an adviser to the Trump administration, from entering Brazil, citing reciprocity over the continued blocking of visas for Health Minister Alexandre Padilha and his family. The move followed the revocation of Beattie’s visa by Brazil’s Foreign Ministry due to incomplete and false information about the trip’s true purpose—visiting jailed former president Jair Bolsonaro—despite the original justification being attendance at the Brazil-U.S. Critical Minerals Forum in São Paulo.
